title: Ceropegia tenuior
taxonomy: Ceropegia tenuior belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ceropegia, which is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Ceropegia is a part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which comprises about 100 genera of flowering plants. Ceropegia tenuior is native to southern Africa and is also found in other parts of the continent. The plant is often cultivated for its unique and exotic appearance in gardens and indoor spaces.

intro: Ceropegia tenuior, also known as the Devil's Claw or Ceropegia, is a succulent plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Ceropegia tenuior has been used to alleviate various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and gastrointestinal protective properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as harpagoside, harpagide, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Ceropegia tenuior is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat musculoskeletal conditions, arthritis, and other inflammatory diseases.
